_comment: OUTCOME assertion set for the hitl skill (companion to spec.json, which scores ROUTING/coverage). Graded by scripts/grade_skill_outcome.py against a captured run's evidence bundle. Deterministic assertions grade offline (no spend); judge-kind assertions need the live judge (--judge-cmd, ask-before-run) and are SKIPPED otherwise. Honest discriminating assertions for a FAITHFUL bounded human-in-the-loop chunked review of the pinned target: not reverse-engineered to any one capture, so a contaminated/stalled run legitimately scores low. output_file_exists assertions resolve against a bundle outputs/ dir; capture-side output preservation is a tracked follow-up, so today they fail with that evidence rather than passing. SLICE 7: the `opened-chunk-contract` deterministic assertion was REMOVED — it tested whether the RCF matcher reported chunk-contract.md missing, but Slice 7 retired the chunk-contract.md doc-open as a flaky RCF proxy (spec.json now floors on requiredSummaryFragments=['recommended disposition'] + this substance judge). With chunk-contract.md no longer required, that assertion would pass vacuously; the chunk-shape + non-binding-disposition judge assertions now carry 'did the run apply the chunk-contract presentation discipline' directly, which is the honest signal.

assertions.statement: The captured run actually executed the hitl skill (sanity floor before grading the work).

assertions._comment: Recursive glob: hitl writes the queue under a session-id path (.charness/hitl/runtime/<sess>/queue.json), so a root-level output_file_exists would structurally false-fail; output_glob matches it at any depth.
assertions.statement: The run materialized the resumable review queue (queue.json) so the bounded loop is chunked and resumable, not a one-shot answer.
Each presented chunk carried an original-material excerpt, an Agent Assessment, and a Recommended Disposition — the chunk-contract presentation shape, not a flat summary.
Each Recommended Disposition was framed as non-binding (the human decides), not executed as an action the agent already took.
The run paused for the human's approval before advancing to the next chunk (the human-in-the-loop gate), rather than auto-advancing through all chunks.
